wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the OpenPlanter project overview section focused on introducing the recursive-language-model investigation agent. Explain its purpose as a desktop application with CLI capabilities that analyzes heterogeneous datasets through evidence-backed analysis. Document the core architecture combining Tauri 2 desktop app with Python CLI agent, multi-provider AI integration, and investigation workflow system. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining what OpenPlanter does and technical details for experienced developers covering the hybrid desktop/CLI architecture. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including investigation engine, recursive language model, workspace tools, and knowledge graph system. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like cross-dataset entity resolution and evidence chain construction. Document the relationship between desktop GUI, CLI agent, and underlying Rust core library.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the OpenPlanter introduction and purpose section focused on explaining what OpenPlanter is and why it exists. Explain its role as a recursive-language-model investigation agent that analyzes heterogeneous datasets through evidence-backed analysis. Document the core value proposition of helping investigators uncover non-obvious connections across corporate registries, campaign finance records, lobbying disclosures, government contracts, and other data sources. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining the problem OpenPlanter solves and technical details for experienced developers covering the recursive language model approach. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including investigation workflow, entity resolution, and evidence chain construction. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like cross-dataset analysis and relationship discovery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Command-Line Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the CLI command-line interface focused on the argument parser and command-line operations. Document all available command-line flags and options including workspace management (--workspace), model selection (--provider, --model), execution parameters (--max-depth, --max-steps, --timeout), and UI preferences (--headless, --no-tui, --textual). Explain the complete command syntax, parameter combinations, and their effects on agent behavior. Include practical examples of common CLI workflows such as single-task execution (--task), recursive investigations (--recursive), automated scripting scenarios, and batch processing. Address workspace resolution, session management commands (--resume, --list-sessions), and model listing (--list-models). Provide troubleshooting guidance for CLI-specific issues and best practices for different execution environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Three-Pane Interface Layout
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the three-pane interface layout focused on the Tauri application's main user interface architecture. Explain the sidebar panel for session management with session listing, creation, deletion, and credential status display. Document the chat pane for conversational AI interaction with message history, token tracking, and streaming responses. Detail the investigation pane for knowledge graph visualization using Cytoscape.js with entity nodes, relationship edges, and interactive filtering. Include practical examples of navigating between panes, managing investigation sessions, and interpreting knowledge graph visualizations. Address responsive design considerations, keyboard shortcuts, and accessibility features. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common interface issues and performance optimization tips for large knowledge graphs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Recursive Execution Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the recursive execution patterns subsection. Explain the core recursive language model architecture, subtask delegation mechanisms, and depth management systems. Document the _solve_recursive method implementation, objective parsing algorithms, and automatic recursion detection. Include practical examples of recursive delegation patterns, depth-first exploration strategies, and convergence criteria. Address the judge model integration for result evaluation, acceptance criteria enforcement, and quality assurance. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common recursion issues, infinite loops, and depth management problems. Include performance optimization techniques for deep recursion scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Provider Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the provider configuration section focused on setting up and managing multiple AI providers. Document the configuration system for OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Cerebras, Z.AI, and Ollama providers including API key management, base URL customization, and environment variable handling. Explain provider selection logic, credential storage patterns, and authentication methods. Include practical examples of provider-specific configurations, endpoint customization, and fallback strategies. Address security considerations for credential management and best practices for production deployments. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common configuration issues and provider connectivity problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the file operations subsection focused on workspace file management capabilities. Document the read_file, write_file, list_files, search_files, and repository mapping functions. Explain path resolution, security constraints preventing path traversal, and file size limitations. Cover the repository map functionality that extracts symbols and metadata from source code files. Include practical examples of file reading with hashline numbering, directory traversal with glob patterns, and repository symbol extraction. Address security policies including interactive command blocking and heredoc restrictions. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common file operation errors and performance optimization tips for large repositories.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wiki Documentation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the wiki documentation system focused on standardized data source documentation. Explain the wiki directory structure, index.md format, and template usage for creating consistent data source documentation. Document the markdown format requirements, cross-reference section structure, and category organization. Include detailed examples of completed wiki entries, template fields, and metadata requirements. Provide practical guidance on writing effective data source documentation, including jurisdiction information, data availability, and integration notes. Address the standardized format for cross-reference potential sections and entity linking. Include troubleshooting guidance for common documentation formatting issues and best practices for maintaining wiki consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Central Florida ICE Workspace
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Central Florida ICE workspace focused on the systematic investigation of ICE detention facilities and operations in the I4 corridor. Explain the multi-faceted approach including detention statistics analysis, procurement monitoring, TRAC database mining, and FOIA strategy implementation. Document the investigation methodology covering facility data extraction, trend analysis, financial sustainability assessment, and cross-referencing between different data sources. Include detailed walkthroughs of the analysis pipeline from raw data ingestion to structured findings. Provide practical guidance on setting up similar investigations, data cleaning procedures, and automated analysis workflows. Address the integration of multiple data sources including government databases, public records, and statistical reports. Include examples of common analytical patterns and decision-making frameworks used in immigration enforcement investigations.

wiki_catalogs.name: IPC Commands
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive IPC command documentation for the Tauri backend. Document all IPC endpoints including agent control commands (start, stop, pause, resume), session management operations (create, load, save, delete), configuration management (get, set, reset), wiki source operations (list, add, remove), initialization procedures, and handoff mechanisms. For each command, specify the method signature, parameter schemas using TypeScript interfaces, response formats, error codes, and usage examples. Include WebSocket event streams for real-time updates, authentication requirements, and security considerations. Provide client-side implementation examples for both Rust and JavaScript integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wiki Template System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the wiki template system focused on the standardized template used for documenting all data sources in OpenPlanter. Explain the template structure, required sections, and formatting guidelines. Document each template field including source name, jurisdiction, access methods, schema details, cross-reference potential, and validation criteria. Include practical examples showing proper template usage and common formatting patterns. Provide guidance on template customization for different data source types and explain the relationship between template fields and investigation workflows. Address template maintenance, updates, and consistency enforcement across all wiki documentation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the getting started section focused on helping users quickly set up and begin using OpenPlanter. Explain installation procedures for all supported platforms (macOS, Windows, Linux), prerequisite requirements (Rust, Node.js, Tauri dependencies), and initial configuration steps. Document the difference between desktop application and CLI agent usage, including quickstart tutorials for both interfaces. Include step-by-step setup instructions for API key configuration, workspace initialization, and first investigation tutorial. Provide practical examples demonstrating basic investigation workflows and common commands. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring experienced users can quickly get productive.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the OpenPlanter system architecture focused on the hybrid desktop/CLI design pattern. Describe the high-level design combining Tauri 2 desktop application with Python CLI agent, explaining the separation of concerns between the Rust core library, TypeScript frontend, and Python agent components. Document component interactions showing how the desktop app communicates with the Rust core library through IPC commands, while the CLI agent operates independently. Include data flows between the GUI, agent engine, workspace tools, and knowledge graph system. Explain technical decisions behind choosing this hybrid architecture, trade-offs between desktop GUI and CLI flexibility, and constraints imposed by the multi-provider AI integration. Document the technology stack including Rust/Tauri for desktop, Python for CLI, and various AI provider integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Terminal User Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the terminal user interface functionality focused on the Rich REPL and Textual-based TUI. Explain the interactive chat interface, slash command system (/help, /quit, /clear), and real-time conversation flow. Document the difference between Rich REPL (with colors and spinners) and plain REPL modes, including when to use each. Cover the Textual-based TUI with wiki knowledge graph panel integration, keyboard shortcuts, and navigation patterns. Include practical examples of interactive investigations, command usage, and session management through the terminal interface. Address demo mode for anonymized output, terminal compatibility requirements, and performance considerations for different terminal types. Provide troubleshooting guidance for terminal rendering issues and integration with different terminal emulators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Graph Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the knowledge graph visualization system focused on the Cytoscape.js implementation. Explain entity categorization with color coding, node sizing based on importance, and relationship edge types. Document interactive features including zoom controls, panning, node highlighting, and contextual popups with entity details. Detail filtering capabilities by entity type, relationship strength, and temporal scope. Include practical examples of interpreting graph patterns, identifying key relationships, and navigating complex investigations. Address performance optimization for large graphs, memory management, and real-time updates during active investigations. Provide guidance on customizing visualization themes, exporting graph data, and integrating with external analysis tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Budget Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the budget management subsection. Explain the token budget system, cost tracking mechanisms, and budget extension algorithms. Address budget exhaustion handling, emergency budget extensions, and cost control measures. wiki_catalogs.name: Model Abstraction Layer

Address the Rust implementation patterns and their integration with the Python layer. Provide guidance on debugging model interactions and optimizing the abstraction layer performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workspace Tools System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the workspace tools system section focused on the 20+ workspace operations available to investigations. Explain the tool architecture including dataset ingestion, file operations, shell execution, web search, and Chrome MCP integration. Document each tool's capabilities, safety constraints, and usage patterns. Include detailed examples of tool compositions for common investigation workflows, security considerations for shell execution, and web search integration with multiple providers. Provide practical guidance on tool selection, parameter configuration, and result interpretation. Address the Rust implementations of core tools and their integration with the Python agent. Include troubleshooting guidance for tool execution issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Security Policies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the tool security policies subsection focused on runtime safety mechanisms and policy enforcement. Document the security architecture including path resolution validation, interactive command blocking, heredoc restrictions, and parallel write coordination. Explain the policy enforcement mechanisms, threat mitigation strategies, and sandboxing approaches. Cover the execution scope management, parallel write claims, and conflict detection systems. Include practical examples of policy violations, security alerts, and safe operation patterns. Address security considerations for different tool categories, privilege management, and audit logging. Provide guidance on configuring security policies, troubleshooting policy conflicts, and maintaining secure investigation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lobbying Disclosures Sources
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for lobbying disclosure data sources documentation. Explain the structure and content of Senate lobbying disclosure wiki entries including LD-1/LD-2 filings. Document lobbying activity schemas, client identification methods, issue advocacy patterns, and lobbyist registration requirements. Include practical examples of lobbying analysis workflows, influence mapping, and issue advocacy tracking. Address disclosure gaps, lobbying activity timing, and indirect lobbying strategies. Provide guidance on identifying potential conflicts of interest, analyzing policy impact, and understanding democratic accountability mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Graph System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the knowledge graph system section focused on the wiki-based entity relationship discovery. Explain the cross-reference extraction process, entity resolution algorithms, and fuzzy matching capabilities. Document the standardized wiki format, source documentation structure, and knowledge graph visualization using Cytoscape.js. Include detailed examples of graph construction, entity categorization, and relationship discovery workflows. Provide practical guidance on wiki contribution, template usage, and graph interpretation. Address the Rust implementation of graph processing and its integration with the Python agent. Include troubleshooting guidance for graph construction issues and optimization techniques for large-scale investigations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Nonprofit Sources
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for nonprofit data sources documentation. Explain the structure and content of IRS 990 and nonprofit explorer wiki entries. Document nonprofit schemas, tax-exempt status classifications, revenue thresholds, and program activity reporting requirements. Include practical examples of nonprofit analysis workflows, grantmaking pattern identification, and organizational transparency assessment. Address data quality issues, Form 990 complexity, and nonprofit sector dynamics. Provide guidance on detecting potential misconduct, evaluating organizational effectiveness, and understanding nonprofit influence networks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the configuration management section focused on credential handling, persistent settings, and environment variable management. Explain the configuration hierarchy from CLI flags to environment variables to persistent settings. Document credential storage, encryption, and security considerations. Include detailed examples of configuration scenarios, workspace-specific settings, and provider customization. Provide practical guidance on configuration best practices, troubleshooting configuration issues, and migrating between environments. Address the relationship between desktop application settings and CLI agent configuration. Include security considerations for credential management and backup strategies for configuration data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sanctions Lists Sources
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for sanctions list data sources documentation. Explain the structure and content of OFAC SDN list wiki entries and other sanctions regime documentation. Document sanction schemas, entity classification systems, secondary sanctions considerations, and geographic scope limitations. Include practical examples of sanctions screening workflows, debarment detection, and transaction risk assessment. Address data refresh cycles, legal interpretations, and compliance obligations. Provide guidance on understanding sanctions evasion tactics, evaluating exposure risks, and implementing effective screening programs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Sources Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the data sources documentation section focused on the standardized wiki format for documenting external data sources. Explain the wiki structure, template usage, and contribution guidelines for adding new data sources. Document the categorized data source types including campaign finance, contracts, corporate registries, financial data, infrastructure, international offshore data, lobbying disclosures, nonprofits, regulatory databases, and sanctions lists. Include detailed examples of wiki documentation patterns, cross-referencing strategies, and source validation processes. Provide practical guidance on wiki maintenance, updating procedures, and quality assurance for data source documentation. Address the relationship between wiki documentation and investigation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: International & Offshore Sources
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for international and offshore data sources documentation. Explain the structure and content of ICIJ Offshore Leaks database wiki entries and other international transparency initiatives. Document offshore entity schemas, beneficial ownership identification, tax haven classifications, and cross-border financial flow patterns. Include practical examples of international investigation workflows, wealth attribution analysis, and jurisdictional complexity navigation. Address data privacy considerations, legal restrictions, and international cooperation challenges. Provide guidance on navigating complex ownership structures, detecting tax avoidance schemes, and understanding global financial networks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Investigation Workspaces
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the investigation workspaces section focused on real-world investigation examples and workspace organization. Explain the structure and analysis workflows demonstrated in the central Florida ICE workspace, Jon Maples opposition research, and Seminole Scout public records investigation. Document the research methodologies, data analysis approaches, and evidence synthesis processes used in each workspace. Include detailed walkthroughs of investigation planning, data ingestion, entity resolution, and finding generation. Provide practical guidance on workspace setup, data organization, and investigation methodology. Address the relationship between workspace structure and investigation effectiveness. Include examples of common investigation patterns and lessons learned from real cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure & Census Sources
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for infrastructure and census data sources documentation. Explain the structure and content of US Census American Community Survey wiki entries and other demographic data sources. Document demographic schemas, geographic hierarchies, socioeconomic indicators, and temporal change analysis methods. Include practical examples of community analysis workflows, demographic trend identification, and geographic targeting strategies. Address data aggregation methods, sampling limitations, and demographic proxy construction. Provide guidance on understanding community characteristics, identifying vulnerable populations, and analyzing social determinants of health and wellbeing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the development guide section focused on building, testing, and contributing to OpenPlanter. Explain the development environment setup for both desktop application and CLI agent, including Rust, Node.js, and Python dependencies. Document the testing framework covering unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing. Include detailed build instructions for development mode, production builds, and cross-platform compilation. Provide practical guidance on debugging techniques, code organization, and contribution workflows. Address the Docker-based development environment and continuous integration setup. Include troubleshooting guidance for common development issues and best practices for extending the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for data source contribution guidelines focused on the process for adding new data sources to the OpenPlanter wiki. Explain the contribution workflow from template copying to final integration, including quality review processes and documentation standards. Document the relationship between wiki documentation and investigation workflows, cross-referencing requirements, and validation procedures. Include practical examples of successful contributions, common pitfalls to avoid, and best practices for maintaining wiki quality. Address version control considerations, collaborative editing workflows, and community moderation processes. Provide guidance on source verification, data integrity assurance, and long-term maintenance responsibilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the OpenPlanter IPC command system and tool definitions. Document Tauri IPC commands for agent control, wiki management, and configuration operations including method signatures, parameter schemas, and response formats. Include comprehensive tool definition reference covering all 20+ workspace tools with JSON schemas, parameter specifications, and usage examples. Document the model interface abstraction, provider configuration endpoints, and session management APIs. Provide practical examples of programmatic access patterns, error handling strategies, and integration approaches. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and migration guidance. Include client implementation guidelines for both desktop and CLI integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the deployment and operations section focused on production deployment, containerization, and operational management. Explain Docker-based deployment using docker-compose, container configuration, and volume management. Document the GitHub Actions workflows for automated testing and release management. Include practical guidance on environment configuration, scaling considerations, and monitoring approaches. Address security considerations for production deployments, credential management, and access control. Provide troubleshooting guidance for deployment issues, performance optimization, and maintenance procedures. Include backup strategies, disaster recovery planning, and operational best practices for running OpenPlanter in production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
Based on my analysis of the OpenPlanter project, I can now generate a comprehensive overview:

<blog>

# OpenPlanter: A Recursive-Language-Model Investigation Agent

## Project Introduction

OpenPlanter is a sophisticated investigation agent that combines artificial intelligence with human expertise to analyze complex datasets and uncover hidden connections. The project serves as a powerful tool for researchers, journalists, and investigators who need to process heterogeneous data sources including corporate registries, campaign finance records, lobbying disclosures, government contracts, and more.

### Purpose Statement
OpenPlanter operates autonomously with file I/O, shell execution, web search, and recursive sub-agent delegation to resolve entities across multiple datasets and surface non-obvious connections through evidence-backed analysis.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Multi-source Data Integration**: Seamlessly ingest and analyze diverse datasets from various jurisdictions and domains
- **Entity Resolution**: Cross-reference and resolve entities across different databases and documents
- **Evidence-Based Analysis**: Maintain provenance and traceability of findings throughout the investigation process
- **Recursive Problem Solving**: Decompose complex investigations into manageable sub-tasks with acceptance criteria
- **Human-AI Collaboration**: Provide transparent reasoning processes and interactive interfaces for human oversight

### Target Audience
- Investigative journalists and researchers
- Government transparency advocates
- Academic researchers studying complex systems
- Compliance professionals analyzing regulatory frameworks
- Data analysts working with multi-source datasets

## Technical Architecture

OpenPlanter employs a modular, multi-layered architecture that separates concerns between the agent engine, user interfaces, and data processing components.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Desktop Application] --> B[Tauri Backend]
    A --> C[Frontend Interface]
    D[CLI Agent] --> E[Python Engine]
    B --> F[Rust Core Library]
    C --> G[React Components]
    E --> H[Workspace Tools]
    E --> I[Model Abstraction]
    F --> J[IPC Commands]
    G --> K[Chat Pane]
    G --> L[Graph Pane]
    G --> M[Input Bar]
    H --> N[File Operations]
    H --> O[Web Search]
    H --> P[Shell Execution]
    I --> Q[Provider Integration]
    J --> R[Agent Commands]
    J --> S[Wiki Commands]
    J --> T[Config Commands]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Desktop Application Layer**
- Built with Tauri 2 for cross-platform compatibility
- Three-pane interface: Sidebar (session management), Chat Pane (conversation interface), Knowledge Graph (interactive visualization)
- Real-time knowledge graph rendering with Cytoscape.js

**CLI Agent Layer**
- Pure Python implementation for headless operation
- Rich terminal UI with interactive features
- Support for both flat and recursive problem-solving modes

**Core Engine Layer**
- Recursive Language Model (RLM) engine for autonomous reasoning
- Workspace tool system for file operations, web search, and shell execution
- Session persistence and lifecycle management

**Data Processing Layer**
- Wiki-based knowledge graph system
- Cross-reference extraction and entity resolution
- Multi-provider model integration (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Cerebras, Z.AI, Ollama)

### Design Patterns

**Recursive Delegation Pattern**
The agent can spawn sub-agents to parallelize entity resolution and cross-dataset linking, enabling scalable analysis of large investigations.

**Provider Abstraction Pattern**
Unified interface for multiple AI providers with consistent behavior across different model APIs.

**Tool Composition Pattern**
Modular tool system allowing flexible combinations of workspace operations, web searches, and specialized data processing functions.

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Desktop Application Entry Point**
- `openplanter-desktop/frontend/src/main.ts` - React-based frontend with state management
- `openplanter-desktop/crates/op-tauri/src/main.rs` - Tauri backend with IPC command handlers

**CLI Agent Entry Point**
- `agent/__main__.py` - Command-line interface with argument parsing and session management

### Core Modules

**Agent Engine**
- `agent/engine.py` - Recursive language model engine with budget management and progress tracking
- `agent/runtime.py` - Session persistence, lifecycle management, and state serialization

**Model Integration**
- `agent/model.py` - Provider-agnostic model abstraction
- `agent/builder.py` - Factory for creating model instances
- `agent/config.py` - Configuration management with environment variable support

**Workspace Tools**
- `agent/tools.py` - Comprehensive tool implementations for file operations, web search, shell execution, and Chrome MCP integration
- `agent/tool_defs.py` - JSON schemas defining tool interfaces

**Knowledge Graph System**
- `agent/wiki_graph.py` - Wiki-based knowledge graph with cross-reference extraction
- `wiki/index.md` - Standardized documentation for data sources

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `.env` files with provider API keys and runtime settings
- Workspace-specific configuration with hierarchical resolution
- Persistent settings stored in `.openplanter/settings.json`

**Build System**
- `pyproject.toml` - Python package configuration with dependencies
- `openplanter-desktop/Cargo.toml` - Rust workspace configuration
- `docker-compose.yml` - Container orchestration for deployment

### External Dependencies

**Python Dependencies**
- `rich` - Enhanced console output and formatting
- `prompt_toolkit` - Interactive command-line interface
- `pyfiglet` - ASCII art generation for CLI interface

**Rust Dependencies**
- `tauri` - Cross-platform application framework
- `tokio` - Asynchronous runtime for concurrent operations
- `serde` - Serialization for configuration and data exchange

**Development Dependencies**
- `pytest` - Testing framework with async support
- `networkx` - Graph analysis for knowledge representation
- `numpy` - Numerical computing for data processing

### Integration Points

**Chrome DevTools MCP Integration**
- Native Chrome automation through Chrome DevTools Message Computation Protocol
- Automatic discovery and integration of browser tools into the agent's toolkit

**External API Integrations**
- Multiple web search providers (Exa, Firecrawl, Brave, Tavily)
- Document AI and OCR services through Mistral integration
- Audio transcription services for interview analysis

## Key Features

### Investigation Workflow

**Recursive Problem Decomposition**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Analysis
    Analysis --> Subtask: Complex objective
    Subtask --> Verification: Subtask complete
    Verification --> Synthesis: Evidence collected
    Synthesis --> Analysis: New insights
    Analysis --> Finalization: Clear conclusion
    Finalization --> [*]
```

**Multi-Provider Model Support**
- OpenAI (Azure Foundry proxy)
- Anthropic (Claude models)
- OpenRouter (multi-provider routing)
- Cerebras (high-throughput inference)
- Z.AI (PayGo and Coding plans)
- Ollama (local models)

### Knowledge Graph Visualization

**Interactive Entity Mapping**
- Color-coded nodes representing different data categories
- Real-time graph updates as investigation progresses
- Slide-out drawers for detailed wiki documentation
- Support for force-directed, hierarchical, and circular layouts

### Session Management

**Persistent Investigation State**
- Automatic checkpointing of investigation progress
- Resume capability for interrupted sessions
- Timeline of completed turns and observations
- Evidence collection and artifact management

### Advanced Capabilities

**Cross-Reference Analysis**
- Automated extraction of entity references from wiki documents
- Fuzzy matching for entity resolution across datasets
- Confidence scoring for cross-dataset relationships
- Visual representation of discovered connections

**Retrieval-Augmented Generation**
- Semantic search over embedded knowledge bases
- Configurable embedding providers (Voyage, Mistral)
- Dynamic retrieval packet injection into prompts
- Local embedding index management

**Audio Processing Pipeline**
- Automatic transcription of interviews and recordings
- Speaker diarization and timestamp extraction
- Chunked processing for long-form content
- Integration with Mistral's offline transcription API

Sources:
- [README.md](README.md)
- [agent/__main__.py](agent/__main__.py)
- [agent/engine.py](agent/engine.py)
- [openplanter-desktop/frontend/src/main.ts](openplanter-desktop/frontend/src/main.ts)
- [openplanter-desktop/Cargo.toml](openplanter-desktop/Cargo.toml)
- [wiki/index.md](wiki/index.md)
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile)
- [agent/tools.py](agent/tools.py)
- [agent/wiki_graph.py](agent/wiki_graph.py)
- [agent/runtime.py](agent/runtime.py)

</blog>
